justification: All pass criteria met: MD5 identified as cryptographically broken with specific references to collision/preimage attacks and NIST/PCI-DSS prohibitions; HMAC-SHA256 explicitly recommended as replacement; timing attack via === clearly identified with exploitation method described; crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with correct usage; timestamp design flaw thoroughly documented as a functional break; safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: MD5 finding is exceptionally detailed — mentions collision attacks, preimage attacks, length-extension vulnerability, and regulatory prohibitions
Timing attack explanation includes practical exploitation steps (progressive prefix matching, response-time measurement)
Timestamp issue correctly elevated to Critical as a functional break, not just a design concern
Identifies the hash-vs-HMAC distinction explicitly — notes that naive payload+key concatenation is vulnerable to length-extension attacks
Suggests JWT as a standard alternative, which addresses the self-contained token format issue
Production readiness section covers migration concerns (existing tokens become invalid) which shows operational maturity
Correctly notes the need for a length check before timingSafeEqual
